ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR First Amended Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Amendment; For Relief Under Probate Code Section 850, Et Seq; And Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jul-24-2024 Continued To Aug-07-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle appeared by zoom for Dawn LeBlanc and her children.
Edward Koplowitz appeared by zoom for Ankit Sahu (trustee/respondent).
Lynn Searle appeared by zoom for Charles Waddell, Jr.
William Broderick-Villa appeared by zoom for Dana LeBlanc.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that the parties proceeded to mediation, that a settlement in principle was reached and that a settlement agreement is in circulation. Mr. Koplowitz also stated that the parties are in the process of working out their differences and suggested a 30-day continuance. The First Amended Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendment, etc. is continued to August 27, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR First Amended Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Amendment; For Relief Under Probate Code Section 850, Et Seq; And Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Continued To Jul-24-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle appeared by zoom for and with Dawn Leblanc in person.
Lynn Searle appeared by zoom for Charles Waddell (beneficiary).
Edward Koplowitz appeared by zoom for Ankit Sahu (trustee).
William Broderick-Villa appeared by zoom for and with Dana Leblanc by zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the matter not being ready to set for trial, Mrs. Epstein having made herself available for further mediation, there being a lot of discovery still pending, the parties having participated in two sessions of mediation, the need to amend the petition, continuing the matter for 90-days, the parties providing a stipulation and order to file an amended petition, etc. The First Amended Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendment, etc., and the trial setting conference are continued to July 24, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Guillory. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Accounting
Off Calendar Jan-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Alexander Bidddle appeared via zoom for Dawn Leblanc (petitioner).
Ed Koplowitz appeared via zoom for and with Ankit Sahu (trustee)
Lynn Searle appeared via zoom for Charles Waddell.

Discussion was held regarding mediation, that Mr. Biddle stated it was unsuccessful, that the money changes and with different financial institutions, that Mr. Biddle's client walked out during meditation, and that all parties are ready for a trial date sometime in June.

The Court expressed that it needs to determine who are the rightful successor-in-interest from the pending petition. Petition to compel accounting is denied without prejudice.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR First Amended Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Amendment; For Relief Under Probate Code Section 850, Et Seq; And Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Dec-13-2023 Continued To Apr-17-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Bidddle appeared via zoom for Dawn Leblanc (petitioner).
Ed Koplowitz appeared in person for Ankit Sahu (trustee)
Lynn Searle appeared via zoom for Charles Waddell.

Mr. Biddle stated that there is insufficient knowledge of other assets, that there is a petition to compel on and would like to have it continued. The Court issues sanctions in the amount of $250.00 against Edward Koplowitz and Lynn Searle payable within 10 days of the order. The hearing set on December 27, 2023 for Petition to compel accounting is continued to January 24, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. First amended petition to determine validity of purported trust amendment etc., is continued to April 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR First Amended Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Amendment; For Relief Under Probate Code Section 850, Et Seq; And Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Continued To Dec-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Alexander Biddle, Esq., via Zoom, for Dawn LeBlanc, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court recognizes absences of Mr. Koplowitz, counsel for Trustee, and Ms. Searle, counsel for Objector. Mr. Biddle reports on the status of the matter and at the court's query, reports that the possibility of the parties continuing settlement discussions is currently not viable but that the parties have participated in mediation which did not result in settlement. The court urges counsel to continue to discuss settlement with the opposing counsel.

Upon further discussion, the court will continue the subject matter to Dec-13-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Edward Koplowitz and Lynn Searle to appear on the same date to explain why they failed to appear. An Order to Show Cause as to why the court should not order Edward Koplowitz and Lynn Searle to pay sanctions of $250 each for failure to appear is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cabrera. Not Reported.

Trustees Petition For Order Approving Mediation Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-01-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Edward Koplowitz appearing via zoom for Ankit Sahu (trustee).
Garry Cohen appearing via zoom for Dawn LeBlanc etal (objectors).
Lynn Searle appearing in person for Charles Waddell, Jr (objector).
William Broderick appearing via zoom for and with Dana Leblanc.

Discussion was held regarding global settlement by all parties, that Mr. Cohen's client doesn't agree with the settlement, that Mr. Broderick 's client would like a $130,000.00 buyout as a part of settlement agreement, and that if all parties come to a settlement agreement it would save time and money for all their clients. First amended petition to determine validity of purported trust amendment etc., is continued to October 18, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Trustee's petition for order approving mediation settlement agreement is taken under submission.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition To Modify Trust To Allow Contingency Agreement And To Instruct Trustee
Off Calendar May-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle, for and with Dawn LeBlanc, Petitioner; Ed Koplowitz, via Zoom, for Ankit Sahu, Objector; Kevin Fusch, for Charles Waddell, Objector.

The court and Mr. Biddle discuss the issue of the court being unable to order the trust to bear costs of representation interests not identified in the petition. Mr. Biddle offers advocacy for his petition. The court queries Mr. Biddle whether he is seeking determination from the court to which Mr. Biddle confirms he is not. Mr. Biddle suggests that the court consider granting his client's and her children's request for contingency fee agreement. The court confirms that all except one of Dawn LeBlanc's children are adults. The court offers its position and advises that it intends to take the matter under submission to consider Mr. Biddle's request. Mr. Koplowitz offers testimony in support of his client's position. The court mentions the case of Barefoot v. Jennings and probate code 15409.

After brief, additional discussion, the Petition to Modify Trust, etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision
Off Calendar Feb-28-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle, via ZOOM, for Dawn LeBlanc, Petitioner; Kevin Fusch, via ZOOM, for Charles Waddell, in person, Objector; Edward Koplowitz, via ZOOM, for Ankit Sahu, Objector.

The court queries Mr. Biddle regarding the status of an amended Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision. Mr. Biddle refers to the Petition to Modify Trust, etc. currently set for May-18-2022, and advises that he wanted to wait until that petition is adjudicated to file the amended Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision. The court advises Mr. Biddle that it is considering taking the petition set for hearing today, off calendar. Mr. Biddle requests to keep the matter on calendar. The court does not agree and confirms that counsel will need to file a new petition.

The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision is off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.
